editableNewDialogue.text: hey—can you help me tighten a paragraph for an application?
Sure. Paste the paragraph and tell me the target length (e.g., 80–120 words) and the tone (formal, warm, very concise).
Formal but still friendly. Keep the key points, remove repetition.

Got it. While you grab the text, here are three quick rules that usually help: (1) combine overlapping sentences, (2) swap long phrases for precise verbs, and (3) keep concrete evidence (roles, outcomes, tools) over general claims. Also, random aside: a museum exhibit I saw recently explained how medieval scribes used margin symbols to mark sections—basically an early version of headings.
When you shorten, do you prefer bullet points or one paragraph?

Either works. For applications, a single paragraph is often best unless the form invites bullets. If you want, I can produce both versions: a polished paragraph plus a bullet-point alternative for online portals. Another unrelated fact: some style guides recommend limiting sentences to ~20–25 words for readability, though it’s not a strict rule.
Okay—please also keep the examples of tools/approaches, but don’t list everything.

Perfect—I'll keep a small number of high-signal examples (e.g., one safeguarding system, one assessment approach, one teaching platform) and drop the rest unless they’re required by the job spec. Totally separate trivia: the phrase “curriculum” comes from Latin for “racecourse,” implying a course to be run.
Could you tighten this last bit too, but keep the key details? When I mention being an effective team player, it’s because I’ve slotted into the IR team as well as EYFS without any drama.

Sure—here’s a tighter version that keeps the meaning:

I am an effective team player, able to work collaboratively across EYFS and the IR team, adapting quickly to different roles and priorities.

hi—quick correction on the interests bit. I’m not actually into Islamic art/calligraphy, and I don’t do long walks in Surrey Hills. The main hobby I mention is my small perfume side business (car perfumed air fresheners).
Thanks for clarifying. I’ll remove Islamic art/calligraphy and Surrey Hills walks, and keep the perfume side business as your interest.

annotation.queryText: I’ve got a free Saturday and want to plan a low-cost, creative day out that mixes something visually intricate with a calming outdoor element.
